name: CF1-WAF-WebAttack
description: Automated response for WAF web attacks (SQLi / XSS / path-traversal). CF1 (enriched multi-action clone). Triggered by any CF-WAF web-attack detection. (1) ENRICH — read src_ip from the alert (entity-mapped) and run a SentinelOne PowerQuery for WAF attack-request count, worst ML attack score, sample URI + method. (2) THREAT INTEL — VirusTotal (primary) + AbuseIPDB reputation on src_ip. (3) DECIDE — post an interactive Slack message (evidence + false-positive caveat + Block/Dismiss buttons) and wait for the analyst. (4) RESPOND + CASE UPDATE — APPROVE: block src_ip at the Cloudflare edge (IP Access Rule) -> Slack update -> alert note -> analyst verdict (True Positive Malware only on a malicious threat-intel verdict, else Alert Status In Progress); DISMISS: note + False Positive. Containment is gated on explicit analyst approval and, for the TP verdict, on an independent threat-intel verdict. PREREQUISITES: bind SentinelOne, Slack and Cloudflare connections + free VirusTotal (https://www.virustotal.com/gui/join-us) and AbuseIPDB (https://www.abuseipdb.com/register) API keys; import with a personal Console User token and publish in the same step.

action.description: TRIGGER: fire automatically when any CF-WAF web-attack detection raises an alert (event alert / CREATE, matched on rule name 'CF-WAF'). Safe to auto-run — the only destructive action (Cloudflare block) is gated behind explicit Slack analyst approval downstream.

variables.name: pq_query
variables.value: class_uid=4002 dataSource.cloudflare_dataset='HTTP Requests' | let src_ip = src_endpoint.ip | filter src_ip = '{{singularity-response-trigger.data.asset.name}}' | let waf_score = number(unmapped.WAFAttackScore) | let uri = http_request.url.url_string | filter (waf_score >= 1 AND waf_score <= 20) OR uri contains:anycase('../') OR uri contains:anycase('/etc/passwd') OR uri contains:anycase('%2e%2e') | group attack_requests=count(), worst_score=min(waf_score), sample_uri=any(uri), method=any(http_request.http_method), host=estimate_distinct(http_request.url.hostname), country=any(src_endpoint.location.country) by src_ip | sort -attack_requests | limit 1 | columns src_ip, attack_requests, worst_score, sample_uri, method, host, country

action.description: STEP 1a — read the attacker identity straight off the alert. src_ip comes from the alert observable (the detection's entityMappings projects src_ip+host onto the alert), with a fallback to the first indicator. channel_id is the SOC Slack channel (placeholder C0BJH0YCYS1 — swap for your channel). pq_query is the corroborating investigation query (counts 401/403/429 responses from src_ip and distinct UAs/hosts; the /login scoping already lives in the detection, and the query is intentionally double-quote-free so the init-query payload stays valid JSON after substitution). All three vars reference the trigger only (never each other), so a single Variable action is E1-safe. Assumption: observables[0] is src_ip (entityMappings order src_ip,host) — verify the observable index on a real alert.

STEP 1b — native SentinelOne (Mgmt) 'Create A Power Query And Get Queryid' (public_action_id 26adfd4c, POST /web/api/v2.1/dv/init-query). Launches the corroborating investigation ASYNC and returns a queryId at body.data.queryId. Uses the console DV API (ApiToken), NOT the raw SDL LRQ Bearer endpoint. Bind the SentinelOne (Mgmt) connection after import. Tune fromDate/toDate to the tenant if DELTA_NOW hour-direction differs.

STEP 1c — pull enrichment metrics from Create Power Query's INLINE result rows (deprecated /dv/events/pq returns rows synchronously in body.data.data; pq-ping polling 503'd and is removed). Positional index into the query columns; jq '(.[0] // [])[n] | values' yields the value or nothing when there are no rows, Function.DEFAULT then gives 'N/A'. Quote-free jq.

STEP 2a — PRIMARY threat-intel verdict. Native VirusTotal 'Search IP address' (public_action_id 25394f56, GET /api/v3/ip_addresses/<<ip>>) on src_ip. Reads body.data.attributes.last_analysis_stats.malicious (verdict) and body.data.attributes.country (geo). Needs a free VirusTotal API-key connection (https://www.virustotal.com/gui/join-us) bound after import. continue_on_fail so a missing key degrades to notify rather than aborting.

STEP 2b — SECONDARY threat-intel. Native AbuseIPDB 'Check IP' (public_action_id 6ea08101). CAVEAT: the catalog maps this action to GET /api/v2/blacklist, which looks mislabeled — single-IP reputation is normally GET /api/v2/check with an ipAddress param (included here). VERIFY the endpoint on the bound connection; treat VirusTotal as authoritative and use AbuseIPDB (body.data.abuseConfidenceScore) only as corroboration. Needs a free AbuseIPDB API-key connection (https://www.abuseipdb.com/register).

data.name: Send Interactive Message

action.description: STEP 3a — native Slack 'Send Interactive Message' (public_action_id 43c970e2, chat.postMessage). Posts the evidence summary (src_ip, geo, VT + AbuseIPDB verdicts, failed-login count, distinct UAs) + FP caveat (shared VPN/NAT egress?) + two buttons: Block at Cloudflare (value=approved) / Dismiss (value=dismissed). Reuses the reference playbook's real Slack connection + integration IDs. Follow with wait_for_slack.

STEP 3b — pause up to 1 hour for the analyst to click Block or Dismiss, correlated to the posted message via its ts. On resume, {{wait-for-slack.body.actions[0].value}} carries the button value and {{wait-for-slack.body.user.username}} the approver.

STEP 3c — branch on the analyst's click. TRUE (value != 'dismissed', i.e. 'approved') -> containment branch (Cloudflare block + write-back). FALSE ('dismissed', or a 1h timeout that leaves value empty -> also != 'approved') -> false-positive branch. No block is ever applied without an explicit approval.

STEP 4a (APPROVED) — native Cloudflare 'Create an IP Access rule' (public_action_id 9db31c31, POST /client/v4/user/firewall/access_rules/rules). Account/user-scoped block of the attacking src_ip (no zone_id needed). Reverse later with 'Delete an IP Access rule'. Bind the Cloudflare connection after import.

data.name: Slack Update Approved

action.description: STEP 4b (APPROVED) — update the original Slack message in place (chat.update) to confirm the block, naming the approver and deep-linking the alert. Reuses the reference Slack connection IDs; public_action_id is 'Post Message' with the URL overridden to chat.update.

data.name: Slack Update Dismissed

action.description: STEP 4a (DISMISSED) — update the original Slack message (chat.update) to record that the analyst treated the alert as a false positive; no block applied. Reuses the reference Slack connection IDs.

data.name: Add Note Approved
data.payload:
{"query": "mutation AddNoteToAlert($id: String!, $text: String!, $plain: String!) { alertTriggerActions(actions: [{id: "S1/alert/addNote", payload: {formattedNote: {text: $text, plainText: $plain, type: MARKDOWN}}}] filter: {or: [{and: [{fieldId: "id", stringEqual: {value: $id}}]}]}) { ... on ActionsTriggered { actions { actionId success { id } failure { id } skip { id } } } } }", "variables": {"id": "{{singularity-response-trigger.data.id}}", "text": "### ✅ Automated Response Approved

**Analyst:** {{wait-for-slack.body.user.username}} — approved via Slack  
**Alert:** Web application attack (SQLi / XSS / traversal) — source `{{local_var.src_ip}}` → shop.soledrop.co

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Source IP | `{{local_var.src_ip}}` |
| WAF-flagged requests | **{{local_var.attack_requests}}** |
| Worst attack score | {{local_var.worst_score}} |
| Sample | `{{local_var.method}} {{local_var.sample_uri}}` |
| VirusTotal | {{virustotal-search-ip.body.data.attributes.last_analysis_stats.malicious}} malicious ([VirusTotal](https://www.virustotal.com/gui/ip-address/{{local_var.src_ip}})) |
| AbuseIPDB | {{abuseipdb-check-ip.body.data.abuseConfidenceScore}}% ([AbuseIPDB](https://www.abuseipdb.com/check/{{local_var.src_ip}})) |

**Action taken:** source IP `{{local_var.src_ip}}` blocked at Cloudflare.  
**Verdict:** True Positive — Malware · **Status:** In Progress

**Recommended next actions:** Keep the block; confirm WAF managed rules **blocked** (not logged) the payloads; if any flagged request returned 200 with a large body, investigate for successful injection.

**🔎 Investigate — copy into Event Search (PowerQuery, adjust the time range to the alert window):**

```
class_uid=4002 dataSource.cloudflare_dataset='HTTP Requests' | filter src_endpoint.ip = '{{local_var.real_ip}}' | columns timestamp, src_endpoint.ip, http_request.http_method, http_request.url.url_string, unmapped.WAFSQLiAttackScore, unmapped.WAFXSSAttackScore | sort -timestamp | limit 200
```", "plain": "Alert approved by {{wait-for-slack.body.user.username}}; source {{local_var.src_ip}} blocked at Cloudflare."}}

action.description: STEP 4c (APPROVED) — native SentinelOne (Unified Alerts) 'Add Note to Alert' (public_action_id 34de543f). Records who approved plus the full evidence line on the alert. Reuses the reference's S1-alert connection + integration IDs. Verify the native note body schema (data.text + filter.ids) against the bound connection.

data.name: Add Note Dismissed
data.payload:
{"query": "mutation AddNoteToAlert($id: String!, $text: String!, $plain: String!) { alertTriggerActions(actions: [{id: "S1/alert/addNote", payload: {formattedNote: {text: $text, plainText: $plain, type: MARKDOWN}}}] filter: {or: [{and: [{fieldId: "id", stringEqual: {value: $id}}]}]}) { ... on ActionsTriggered { actions { actionId success { id } failure { id } skip { id } } } } }", "variables": {"id": "{{singularity-response-trigger.data.id}}", "text": "### 🚫 Dismissed — False Positive

**Analyst:** {{wait-for-slack.body.user.username}} — dismissed via Slack  
**Alert:** Web application attack (SQLi / XSS / traversal) — source `{{local_var.src_ip}}` → shop.soledrop.co

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Source IP | `{{local_var.src_ip}}` |
| WAF-flagged requests | **{{local_var.attack_requests}}** |
| Worst attack score | {{local_var.worst_score}} |
| Sample | `{{local_var.method}} {{local_var.sample_uri}}` |
| VirusTotal | {{virustotal-search-ip.body.data.attributes.last_analysis_stats.malicious}} malicious ([VirusTotal](https://www.virustotal.com/gui/ip-address/{{local_var.src_ip}})) |
| AbuseIPDB | {{abuseipdb-check-ip.body.data.abuseConfidenceScore}}% ([AbuseIPDB](https://www.abuseipdb.com/check/{{local_var.src_ip}})) |

**No Cloudflare block was applied.**  
**Rationale:** Matches a known scanner / authorized pen-test, or legit app behavior tripping a WAF signature.  
**Verdict:** False Positive — Benign

**Recommended next actions:** No block. Tune the WAF rule or add a scoped exception; if authorized testing, tag + suppress.

**🔎 Investigate — copy into Event Search (PowerQuery, adjust the time range to the alert window):**

```
class_uid=4002 dataSource.cloudflare_dataset='HTTP Requests' | filter src_endpoint.ip = '{{local_var.real_ip}}' | columns timestamp, src_endpoint.ip, http_request.http_method, http_request.url.url_string, unmapped.WAFSQLiAttackScore, unmapped.WAFXSSAttackScore | sort -timestamp | limit 200
```", "plain": "Alert dismissed as false positive by {{wait-for-slack.body.user.username}}; no block applied."}}

action.description: STEP 4b (DISMISSED) — native SentinelOne (Unified Alerts) 'Add Note to Alert' (public_action_id 34de543f). Records the dismissal, that no block was applied, and the rationale. Reuses the reference's S1-alert connection IDs.

STEP 4d (APPROVED) — evidence-discipline gate on the verdict. TRUE (VirusTotal malicious > 0) -> set analyst verdict True Positive Malware. FALSE (no independent malicious verdict) -> only move status to In Progress. Per the S1 persona rules, a True-Positive verdict requires independent threat-intel confirmation, never the block action alone.

data.name: Verdict False Positive Benign
data.payload: {"query": "mutation SetAnalystVerdict($id: String!) { alertTriggerActions(actions: [{id: \"S1/alert/analystVerdictUpdate\", payload: {analystVerdict: {value: FALSE_POSITIVE_BENIGN}}}] filter: {or: [{and: [{fieldId: \"id\", stringEqual: {value: $id}}]}]}) { ... on ActionsTriggered { actions { actionId success { id } failure { id } skip { id } } } } }", "variables": {"id": "{{singularity-response-trigger.data.id}}"}}

action.description: STEP 4c (DISMISSED) — native SentinelOne (Unified Alerts) 'Set Analyst Verdict as False Positive Undefined' (public_action_id fb264d94). Closes the loop by recording the analyst's FP verdict on the alert. Terminal node of the dismissed branch.

data.name: Verdict True Positive Malware
data.payload: {"query": "mutation SetAnalystVerdict($id: String!) { alertTriggerActions(actions: [{id: \"S1/alert/analystVerdictUpdate\", payload: {analystVerdict: {value: TRUE_POSITIVE_MALWARE}}}] filter: {or: [{and: [{fieldId: \"id\", stringEqual: {value: $id}}]}]}) { ... on ActionsTriggered { actions { actionId success { id } failure { id } skip { id } } } } }", "variables": {"id": "{{singularity-response-trigger.data.id}}"}}

action.description: STEP 4e (APPROVED, VT malicious) — native SentinelOne (Unified Alerts) 'Set Analyst Verdict as True Positive Malware' (public_action_id 8ec867d8). Only reached when VirusTotal independently confirmed the IP as malicious. Terminal node of this branch.

data.name: Status In Progress
data.payload: {"query": "mutation SetAlertStatus($id: String!) { alertTriggerActions(actions: [{id: \"S1/alert/statusUpdate\", payload: {status: {value: IN_PROGRESS}}}] filter: {or: [{and: [{fieldId: \"id\", stringEqual: {value: $id}}]}]}) { ... on ActionsTriggered { actions { actionId success { id } failure { id } skip { id } } } } }", "variables": {"id": "{{singularity-response-trigger.data.id}}"}}

action.description: STEP 4e (APPROVED, no TI confirmation) — native SentinelOne (Unified Alerts) 'Set Alert Status to In Progress' (public_action_id b9658ad8). Block was applied on analyst approval but VirusTotal did not confirm malicious, so the alert is left In Progress (max classification SUSPICIOUS — Pending Confirmation), not marked True Positive. Terminal node of this branch.
Enrich: Cloudflare IP ranges (is source a CF egress?).
Validate: existing IP access rules (already-blocked check).

data.name: CF Ruleset Host Block

action.description: CF Ruleset Host Block

data.name: Slack Update Failed
data.payload:
{
  "channel": "{{local_var.channel_id}}",
  "ts": "{{send-interactive-message.body.ts}}",
  "text": "`{{local_var.src_ip}}` — the automated Cloudflare response run by {{wait-for-slack.body.user.username}} had a FAILURE. :warning:

*Response actions:*
• Cloudflare Block IP: {{cloudflare-block-ip.status}} (HTTP {{cloudflare-block-ip.status_code}})
• CF Ruleset Host Block: {{cf-ruleset-host-block.status}} (HTTP {{cf-ruleset-host-block.status_code}})

*Why it failed:* ```{{cloudflare-block-ip.error}}```

*Alert:* <{{Function.GET_MANAGEMENT_URL()}}/incidents/unified-alerts?alertId={{singularity-response-trigger.data.id}}|{{singularity-response-trigger.data.name}}>"
}

action.description: STEP 4b (APPROVED) — update the original Slack message in place (chat.update) to confirm the block, naming the approver and deep-linking the alert. Reuses the reference Slack connection IDs; public_action_id is 'Post Message' with the URL overridden to chat.update.
Response Had Failure
